title: AI Delivery Associate
industry: AI / agentic AI for financial services
requirements_met: Early-career delivery experience in tech project coordination or operations: ran ops/community workstreams and project delivery at Minima
Strong organizational skills and proactive communication: coordinated cross-functional programmes, investor updates, PR
Comfortable with ambiguity / works without fully defined processes: 6 years bringing structure to early-stage chaos
Data literacy with dashboards and spreadsheets: built metric dashboards, data analysis at Amazon Alexa (98% success)
Clear written and verbal communication: content, PR, investor relations
gaps: Listing values technical curiosity around APIs and integrations; Ryan has hands-on AI-agent building (Python/TS) but limited formal enterprise API-integration delivery experience
preferred_met: Configuring the AI agent for specific use cases: actively builds and configures AI agents with Claude Code
Monitor performance data and identify improvements: failure/data analysis background

experience_fit.calculation: Role is pitched early-career; Ryan at ~8yrs is above the band but strongly qualified for the delivery/ops content = 12/15

industry_match.calculation: Gradient Labs builds an AI agent for financial services - genuine agentic AI company = 15

location_match.calculation: London hybrid; within user's accepted modes - conservatively scored to reflect commute; (hybrid would normally earn 10)

notes: Gradient Labs builds an AI agent for financial services (customer support automation). AI Delivery Associate owns onboarding/implementation delivery, configures the AI agent, monitors performance - a strong AI-ops fit for Ryan's ops + AI-agent-building background. Agentic AI (TOP choice). Note: prior agent-memory flagged Gradient Labs as engineering-only - this Delivery Associate role is the exception (non-engineering delivery/ops).

must_haves.req: Some experience in project coordination / implementation / customer-facing ops, ideally at a tech company
must_haves.note: Ryan has community/GTM/founder-ops coordination, not classic delivery/implementation; transferable but not a direct match to onboarding-delivery archetype

must_haves.req: Organizational skills — track moving parts, follow through, communicate proactively
must_haves.note: Ran ambassador programmes, hackathon series, investor updates, cross-functional workstreams

must_haves.req: Comfortable with ambiguity, sound judgment without defined processes
must_haves.note: 6 yrs bringing structure to early-stage chaos as first employee

must_haves.req: Technical literacy — comfortable learning APIs/integrations, hold a conversation with technical teams
must_haves.note: Builds AI agents with Claude Code, node-runner network ops, Alexa data analysis — literacy not expertise, which is exactly what's asked

must_haves.req: Data competency — dashboards and spreadsheets to track performance
must_haves.note: Built metric dashboards, segmentation/A/B testing, Alexa query analysis
Content, PR, investor-facing comms across audiences

met_strengths: Explicitly entry/early-career — no years gate; engineering NOT required (literacy only)
UK-remote, no visa gate — Ryan UK-authorized
AI-agent configuration is a core task and Ryan actively builds/configures agents
interview_match.one_line_verdict: Strong skills overlap and no hard blockers; the one soft gap is that 'implementation/delivery at a tech company' archetype differs from Ryan's community/GTM ops background — credible interview, not a lock.
